question: A student scored 360 marks out of 450. What percentage of marks did he score?
A factory produced 840 good items out of 1000 total items. What is the percentage of good items produced?
In a survey of 500 people, 325 preferred online shopping. What percentage preferred online shopping?
A company completed 72 projects out of its target of 90 projects. What percentage of the target was achieved?
Out of 240 employees, 180 attended a training program. What percentage attended the program?
A shop sold 450 units out of 600 units in stock. What percentage of stock was sold?
In an examination, 420 out of 600 students passed. What percentage of students passed?
A cricket player scored 96 runs out of a possible 120 runs. What percentage of runs did he score?
Out of 800 registered voters, 600 cast their votes. What percentage of voters participated?
A warehouse delivered 540 packages out of 720 scheduled deliveries. What percentage was completed?
A student answered 54 questions correctly out of 60. What percentage of questions were correct?
Out of 350 books in a library, 280 are available. What percentage of books are available?
A salesman achieved sales of ₹45,000 against a target of ₹60,000. What percentage of the target was achieved?
Out of 125 students, 100 submitted their assignments on time. What percentage submitted on time?
A machine produced 950 acceptable products out of 1000 products. What percentage were acceptable?
A student attended 180 classes out of 200 conducted classes. What was his attendance percentage?
Out of 160 workers, 120 completed safety training. What percentage completed the training?
A company received 480 positive reviews out of 600 total reviews. What percentage of reviews were positive?
A football team won 18 matches out of 24 played. What percentage of matches did it win?
Out of 90 candidates interviewed, 45 were selected. What percentage were selected?
A student solved 64 problems out of 80 assigned problems. What percentage did he solve?
A hospital successfully treated 270 patients out of 300 admitted patients. What percentage were successfully treated?
Out of 400 houses surveyed, 320 had internet connections. What percentage had internet connections?
A runner completed 21 km of a 28 km marathon. What percentage of the marathon was completed?
Out of 50 questions in a test, a student answered 40 correctly. What percentage of answers were correct?
A university conducted an entrance examination for 2,400 candidates. Out of them, 1,872 candidates qualified for the next round. What percentage of candidates qualified?
A software company assigned 850 bug-fixing tasks to its development team. By the end of the sprint, 697 tasks were completed. What percentage of the assigned tasks were completed?
A school library purchased 1,250 books this year. Among them, 975 books were issued at least once during the year. What percentage of the newly purchased books were issued?
During a vaccination drive, 3,500 residents were registered and 2,870 residents received the vaccine. What percentage of registered residents were vaccinated?
A manufacturing unit produced 4,800 electronic components. Quality inspection approved 3,936 components. What percentage passed the inspection?
A student attempted 180 questions in various mock tests over a week and answered 144 correctly. What percentage of the attempted questions were answered correctly?
A logistics company scheduled 960 deliveries during a month. Due to operational issues, only 768 deliveries were completed on time. What percentage were completed on time?
An online course enrolled 1,500 students. By the end of the course, 1,185 students completed all modules. What percentage successfully completed the course?
A company surveyed 2,250 customers regarding a new product. Out of them, 1,845 expressed satisfaction with the product. What percentage of customers were satisfied?
A cricket player scored 492 runs in a season where his target was 600 runs. What percentage of the target did he achieve?
A college recorded attendance data for a semester. Out of 220 working days, a student attended 178 days. What was the student's attendance percentage?
A factory received an order for 6,000 units. By the delivery deadline, it had produced 4,860 units. What percentage of the order was completed?
An insurance company processed 3,750 claims in a quarter. Out of these, 3,000 claims were settled successfully. What percentage of claims were settled?
A warehouse stored 2,800 products. During an audit, 2,296 products were found in perfect condition. What percentage were in perfect condition?
A mobile application was downloaded by 18,000 users. Among them, 14,580 users remained active after one month. What percentage of users remained active?
A recruitment drive attracted 950 applicants. After screening, 760 candidates were shortlisted. What percentage of applicants were shortlisted?
A bank targeted opening 4,500 new accounts in a financial year. It successfully opened 3,645 accounts. What percentage of the target was achieved?
Out of 5,200 manufactured batteries, 4,264 passed all quality tests. What percentage of batteries passed the tests?
A city's public transport department recorded 7,500 daily passengers. On a particular day, 6,075 passengers used the service. What percentage of the usual daily passengers used the service?
A digital marketing campaign reached 12,500 users. Out of them, 10,250 users clicked on the advertisement. What was the click-through percentage?
A school organized a science exhibition where 680 out of 850 students participated. What percentage of students participated?
A project manager estimated that 1,200 tasks would be completed in a quarter. The team completed 972 tasks. What percentage of the planned tasks were completed?
A coaching institute enrolled 900 students for a certification program. At the end of the course, 738 students passed the final examination. What percentage passed?
A retail store expected sales of ₹8,000,000 during a festive season. Actual sales reached ₹6,480,000. What percentage of the target sales was achieved?
An airline scheduled 1,450 flights during a month. Out of these, 1,189 flights departed on time. What percentage of flights departed on time?
A university conducted an entrance examination for 2,400 candidates. Out of them, 1,872 candidates qualified for the next round. What percentage of candidates qualified?
A software company assigned 850 bug-fixing tasks to its development team. By the end of the sprint, 697 tasks were completed. What percentage of the assigned tasks were completed?
A school library purchased 1,250 books this year. Among them, 975 books were issued at least once during the year. What percentage of the newly purchased books were issued?
During a vaccination drive, 3,500 residents were registered and 2,870 residents received the vaccine. What percentage of registered residents were vaccinated?
A manufacturing unit produced 4,800 electronic components. Quality inspection approved 3,936 components. What percentage passed the inspection?
A student attempted 180 questions in various mock tests over a week and answered 144 correctly. What percentage of the attempted questions were answered correctly?
A logistics company scheduled 960 deliveries during a month. Due to operational issues, only 768 deliveries were completed on time. What percentage were completed on time?
A company's revenue increased by 25% in the first year and then decreased by 20% in the second year. Compared to the original revenue, the final revenue is:
options: Exactly equal to the original revenue
explanation: Let original revenue be 100. After 25% increase = 125. After 20% decrease = 125 × 0.8 = 100. Final = original. Difference = 0%.

question: A student scored 72% marks and failed by 18 marks. If the passing percentage is 75%, what are the maximum marks?
The population of a town increases by 10% every year. If the current population is 24,200, what was the population two years ago?
An item is marked 40% above cost price and then sold at a discount of 20% on the marked price. The seller's profit is:
A company had 800 employees. Due to expansion, the workforce increased by 25%. Later, 10% of the new workforce resigned. How many employees remained?
A candidate answered 80% of the questions correctly and 10% incorrectly. The remaining 20 questions were left unanswered. How many questions were there in total?
The price of sugar increased by 25%. By what percentage should consumption be reduced so that expenditure remains unchanged?
options: Cannot be determined without the original price

question: A trader sells two articles at the same selling price. One article gives a profit of 20% and the other a loss of 20%. Overall, the trader:
explanation: Equal SP with equal gain/loss percentages results in net loss = 4%.

question: In an examination, 35% students failed and 390 students passed. The total number of students who appeared for the exam was:
A village population decreases by 10% every year. If the current population is 7,290, what was it one year ago?
A product is sold after successive discounts of 20% and 10%. The equivalent single discount is:
The value of an investment rises by 50% and then falls by 40%. Compared to the original investment, the final value is:
A candidate secures 45% marks and fails by 30 marks. Another candidate secures 55% marks and gets 50 marks more than the pass marks. The maximum marks are:
A company's expenses are 80% of its income. By what percentage do expenses need to increase to become equal to income?
The number of boys in a school is 25% more than the number of girls. What percentage of the students are boys?
A man spends 75% of his salary. If his salary increases by 20% and his expenditure remains unchanged, what percentage of the new salary does he save?
explanation: Original saving = 25. New salary = 120. Saving = 45. Saving% = 45/120 × 100 = 37.5%.

question: The price of petrol decreases by 20%. A consumer increases consumption by 25%. The expenditure on petrol becomes:
A student obtained 360 marks and missed the first division by 24 marks. If first division requires 64% marks, the maximum marks are:
explanation: First division marks = 384. Difference = 24 = 4% of total. Total = 600.

question: In a survey, 60% preferred Product A, 25% preferred Product B and the remaining 150 people preferred Product C. Total respondents were:
The population of a city increases by 20% and then decreases by 20%. The final population compared to the original is:
A company achieved 85% of its annual target. To achieve the full target, the additional work required is what percentage of the work already completed?
The income of a person is increased by 25% and expenditure by 15%. If originally he saved 20% of his income, the new saving percentage is closest to:
explanation: Income=100, Exp=80. New income=125, New exp=92. Saving=33. Saving%=33/125×100=26.4%, closest to 24%.

question: A test consists of 200 questions. A student answers 70% correctly and 15% incorrectly. How many questions remain unanswered?
